title: Living in Boulder Vlog 2 - 7 - 2017: 100 feet above the middle of 40,000 people

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from his Boulder room on February 7, 2017, using a green screen setup to show aerial density visualizations of Boulder's population. He talks about sleeping 12 hours, adding fans to his bike, planning to sell his car, ordering a new microphone to fix audio quality issues, and spending the day cleaning and organizing his room.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: enthusiastic about showing different backgrounds each day
necessary fix after bad audio from computer fan noise
plans to sell it next week
loves the acoustics in his current room

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-Log for the 7th of February. Here's my green screen show number two. I'm wearing my green t-shirt. Just a random green t-shirt I have. It'd be cool to get a normal green outfit and all you see is my head and nothing will get in the way. You can show stuff up below me.
I should probably do a green outfit until then and see how this does.

So what you see behind me, every day I'll have a different background. So today's background, we're looking at 160,000 people. I'm in the middle of 160,000 people, 100 feet up, which is a good height to be up because that's the height of the statue of Nero in Rome,
Which is also the height of the same sister statue, bike racer level statue, which you actually can see.
The video starts out looking down and then it goes up looking north. The middle of it, I think, is somewhere near downtown, just east of downtown Boulder.
And then you look north, you look at the edge of the 160,000. The green is the outer edge.
The yellow is 40,000 people. And then the very beginning you see 10 and then it goes up to 100 and then yellow.
The red is 100 and then 1,000 and then 10,000, which is a dark light yellow and then 40,000 and then 160,000 people.
So, yeah, panning down looking south, you can see the Great Pyramid of Giza and then the bike racer level building, which is 100 feet high, which is as high as we are.
So today, what did I do? I got up really late. I slept 12 hours.
Today was one of those days, you know, you work as hard as you normally do, but you don't get as much done.
I decided I wanted to get two fans for under my bike. Now I have three fans that can cool me down under my TV.
And then I got more ink to do more printing. Yeah.
Then I'll sell my car. Next week I'll sell my car. That freaked my mom out when I mentioned that.
A lot more stuff. But tomorrow I'm going to get a microphone because yesterday's video, the beginning, the sound was really good.
But then in the second part, the sound was really bad because when the computer is in cool down mode, it makes it sound really bad.
So tomorrow I ordered a mic. So at the Apple store, they said they'd have a mic for me and we'll see how that works.
You know, hopefully it'll record really well even when there's no, you know, be able to cancel out that rattle when the computer is cooling itself.
So, and the acoustics in this room are perfect. I love it. I can sing. I was singing yesterday. I'll include some of that tomorrow.
So, yeah, today was just about cleaning my room. I was just cleaning my room, organizing my room all day busy.
You know, I don't really, I was putting a whole bunch of stuff on the wall, like hooks on the wall and hooking, hanging stuff, utilizing all the space in my room.
I went through a storage thing in my living room that I hadn't even looked at for years and just, you know, closet shopping and that kind of thing.
All right. So let's see. I think that's enough talking. Yeah. 335. All right. Peace out.
Peace out.
